name: hcportal_getLoginHistory
description: Returns the login history for a specified user over the last 365 days. Validates authentication via device-bound access token and logs the request. Returns login timestamps, app versions, device types, and geolocation data (city name, lat/long) ordered most recent first.

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: user's public hasher ID. Validated for non-null and 16-byte DATALENGTH. Used for access token validation and error logging.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token validated via HC.CHECK_PORTAL_ACCESS_TOKEN.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user ID to retrieve login history for. Validated for non-null and 16-byte DATALENGTH. Used in main query WHERE clause.

parameters.name: ipAddress
parameters.description: Client IP address. Used in LOG.GeneralLog message field.

parameters.name: ipGeoDetails
parameters.description: IP geolocation JSON. Stored in LOG.GeneralLog data field. Falls back to placeholder JSON if NULL.

rowsets.description: Returned when validation or authentication fails. Uses errorType codes: 2 (validation failure for publicHasherId or userId), 3 (invalid access token). Multiple error rowsets possible since validation does not short-circuit early—each failed check returns its own error rowset but only the final RETURN exits the SP.

rowsets.name: LoginHistoryResult
rowsets.description: Returned when validation passes. Contains login history records for the user over the last 365 days, ordered by LoginDate descending. Empty rowset if no matching records found.

columns.name: loginDate
columns.description: Timestamp of the login from HC.LaunchAndLogin.LoginDate

columns.name: systemVersion
columns.description: Device OS version from HC.LaunchAndLogin.SystemVersion. COALESCE to empty string.

columns.name: latitude
columns.description: Latitude from HC.City.Latitude (via LEFT JOIN on CityId). COALESCE to 0.0 if NULL.

columns.name: longitude
columns.description: Longitude from HC.City.Longitude (via LEFT JOIN on CityId). COALESCE to 0.0 if NULL.

columns.name: locationName
columns.description: Full city name from HC.City.CityFullName (via LEFT JOIN on CityId). COALESCE to empty string if NULL.

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.ErrorLog on validation/auth failures (lines 39-42, 60-63, 80-83)
INSERT into LOG.GeneralLog on every call after validation (line 96-105), logging IP address, geo details, and error title if failed

codeSmells.detail: SET NOCOUNT ON is present (line 22) but SET XACT_ABORT ON is missing. Runtime errors will not auto-rollback.
The entire SP has no error handling. If queries fail (e.g. FK violations, connection issues), the error propagates unhandled to the caller.

Lines 33-93: All validation blocks run regardless of prior failures. Each failed check SELECTs an error rowset but does not RETURN immediately. @isError flag is checked after all three validations, then single RETURN at line 109. If multiple validations fail, the caller receives multiple error rowsets before the RETURN.

Lines 33, 54: Uses DATALENGTH instead of LEN for validating GUIDs. DATALENGTH returns byte count (16 bytes for UNIQUEIDENTIFIER is correct, but the pattern is inconsistent with general string length checks where LEN should be used). For UNIQUEIDENTIFIER specifically, DATALENGTH(16) is technically correct but the inconsistency is a code smell.
Lines 121-122: Latitude and longitude are COALESCE to 0.0 as sentinel values when City data is missing. Better to use NULL or a flag column to indicate 'no location data'.

codeSmells.issue: Dead input parameters never used in logic
codeSmells.detail: @ipAddress and @ipGeoDetails are accepted as parameters but only used for logging (lines 101-103). They do not affect the SP's core logic or query results.

Line 23: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED allows dirty reads. For a read-only history query this may be acceptable for performance, but could return inconsistent data if writes are happening.
Line 129: Forces query plan recompilation on every call. May be intentional for parameter sniffing mitigation, but adds CPU overhead.

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change errorType integer codes without updating all callers
